description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer who must rotate the organization’s SSH key material through “policy-as-code”.  
    Your assignment:

    1. Generate a **brand-new ED25519 key-pair** in the user’s SSH directory **using a single terminal command** (you may chain shell operators such as “&&” or “;” inside that one command if you need follow-up sub-commands).  
    2. The private key **must** be written to  
       /home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519  
       and the public key automatically saved by ssh-keygen to  
       /home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519.pub
    3. The public-key comment (the text after the key material) **must be exactly**  
       policy-enforced-2023
    4. No pass-phrase may be set (empty string).
    5. Immediately after creating the key-pair, capture its MD5 fingerprint (in the canonical form produced by “ssh-keygen -l -E md5”) in a small audit log located at  
       /home/user/policy_key_creation.log
    6. The log file must contain **exactly three lines, in this fixed order** (no leading/trailing spaces):
       PrivateKeyPath=/home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519  
       PublicKeyPath=/home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519.pub  
       Fingerprint_MD5=<the MD5 fingerprint string produced by ssh-keygen>

       Example of a valid third line (your numbers will differ):
       Fingerprint_MD5=MD5:1a:2b:3c:4d:5e:6f:7g:8h:9i:0j:1k:2l:3m:4n

    7. File-system expectations after successful execution:
       • /home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519               — exists, regular file, permission 600  
       • /home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519.pub           — exists, regular file, readable  
       • The first field of /home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519.pub is “ssh-ed25519”, the last field is “policy-enforced-2023”.  
       • /home/user/policy_key_creation.log           — exists, regular file, exactly 3 lines formatted as shown above, and its fingerprint line matches the key just generated.

    Important constraints:
       • Accomplish everything (key generation **and** log creation) with **one** command you enter in the terminal.  
       • Do not edit any file manually; rely on ssh-keygen and normal shell utilities.  
       • No sudo/root access is available.  
       • The automated tester will validate file existence, permissions, content layout, and that the fingerprint inside the log precisely matches the one returned by “ssh-keygen -l -E md5 -f /home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519.pub”.

    Once the single command completes successfully, the task is finished.
truth:
Required end-state objects for verification:

1. File: /home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519  
   • Type: regular file  
   • Permissions: exactly 600 (-rw-------)  
   • Non-empty

2. File: /home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519.pub  
   • First token: “ssh-ed25519”  
   • Third (comment) token: “policy-enforced-2023”  
   • Contains exactly one line ending with newline

3. File: /home/user/policy_key_creation.log  
   • Exactly three newline-terminated text lines  
     Line 1 literal text: “PrivateKeyPath=/home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519”  
     Line 2 literal text: “PublicKeyPath=/home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519.pub”  
     Line 3 begins with “Fingerprint_MD5=” and the remainder is byte-for-byte identical to the MD5 fingerprint produced by “ssh-keygen -l -E md5 -f /home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519.pub”.  
   • No extra whitespace before or after any line content.

The automated test procedure:

A. Confirm both key files exist and are regular files.  
B. Stat /home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519 to verify mode 600.  
C. Read the public key, split by spaces, assert field[0] == "ssh-ed25519" and field[-1] == "policy-enforced-2023".  
D. Compute expected_fp=$(ssh-keygen -l -E md5 -f /home/user/.ssh/policy_ed25519.pub | awk '{print $2}').  
E. Read /home/user/policy_key_creation.log:  
   • Assert it has exactly three lines with content described above.  
   • Extract fp_log from third line (after “Fingerprint_MD5=”); assert fp_log == $expected_fp.  

If all checks pass, task is graded as successful.
name: task_000000_2983199f
